𝗦𝗜𝗠𝗨𝗧𝗜𝗟 | To provide a fast, terminal-based interface for developers to manage and launch Android emulators and iOS simulators, improving workflow efficiency.  🌟 Rising Fast!

┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 📊 Dart 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: To provide a fast, terminal-based interface for developers to manage and launch Android emulators and iOS simulators, improving workflow efficiency.
📅 Created: Feb 2026 • Updated: Jul 2026

┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ One-Key Launch for emulators/simulators
✅ Custom Android launch options
✅ Shutdown devices
✅ Logcat viewing with filtering
✅ Wireless connection to physical Android devices (ADB Tools)
✅ Custom plugin system for external tools (scrcpy, Maestro, etc.)
✅ Edit configuration file directly from the app


┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐ 935 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 110 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 2 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

SimUtil is a cross-platform terminal UI application for launching, managing, and connecting to Android emulators and iOS simulators. It provides a streamlined interface within the terminal to interact with virtual devices, offering features like one-key launch, custom launch options, and wireless connectivity to physical devices.

┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/dungngminh/simutil

┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅

𝗧𝗔𝗕𝗪𝗥𝗔𝗡𝗚𝗟𝗘𝗥 | To declutter browser tabs, improve performance by closing inactive tabs, and provide easy restoration of closed tabs.  📈 Growing!

✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 📘 TypeScript 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: To declutter browser tabs, improve performance by closing inactive tabs, and provide easy restoration of closed tabs.
📅 Created: Apr 2012 • Updated: Jul 2026

✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Automatic tab closing
✅ Tab Corral for managing and restoring closed tabs
✅ Full session restore with browsing history
✅ Tab Lock to prevent auto-closing
✅ Website exclusion list
✅ Protection for tabs playing audio or in tab groups
✅ Minimum open tabs setting
✅ Right-click menu integration
✅ Pause and resume functionality
✅ Protection for pinned and active tabs


✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐ 629 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 109 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 42 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Tab Wrangler is a browser extension for Chrome and Firefox that automatically closes unused tabs to improve browser performance and reduce clutter. It provides features to manage closed tabs, prevent specific tabs from closing, and exclude certain websites.

✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/tabwrangler/tabwrangler

✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧

𝗜𝗢𝗦 | To provide a dedicated, native mobile and desktop application for users to interact with their Home Assistant smart home setup on Apple devices.  ✨ Notable!

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🍎 Swift 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: To provide a dedicated, native mobile and desktop application for users to interact with their Home Assistant smart home setup on Apple devices.
📅 Created: Apr 2016 • Updated: Jul 2026

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Native iOS, iPadOS, and macOS applications for Home Assistant
✅ Control and monitor smart home devices
✅ Manage automations and scenes
✅ TestFlight beta program for early access
✅ Integration with Apple's developer tools for building and testing


⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐⭐ 2.3K 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 480 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 222 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

This repository provides the official Home Assistant client application for Apple platforms, allowing users to control and monitor their smart home devices from their iPhones, iPads, and Macs. It offers a native experience for managing automations, scenes, and devices.

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/home-assistant/iOS

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

𝗜𝗣𝗕𝗔𝗡 | Protecting servers (dedicated or cloud) on Windows and Linux from unauthorized access attempts by automatically blocking malicious IP addresses.  📈 Growing!

◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🔷 C# 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Protecting servers (dedicated or cloud) on Windows and Linux from unauthorized access attempts by automatically blocking malicious IP addresses.
📅 Created: Feb 2012 • Updated: Jul 2026

◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Automatic IP banning based on failed login attempts
✅ Support for Windows and Linux platforms
✅ Monitors event viewer and log files for security events
✅ Configurable ban thresholds and durations
✅ Supports IPv4 and IPv6
✅ Easy unbanning via unban.txt file
✅ Integration with firewalls (firewalld, nftables, iptables)
✅ Extensible to monitor various applications (RDP, SSH, MySQL, etc.)


◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐⭐ 2.2K 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 400 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 1 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

IPBan is a free, open-source security software for Windows and Linux that automatically blocks IP addresses of attackers attempting to gain unauthorized access to servers. It monitors event logs and application logs for failed login attempts and integrates with firewalls to ban malicious IPs, protecting servers from brute-force attacks and botnets.

◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/DigitalRuby/IPBan
